title: Deployment Environments, OIDC, Workload Identity, and Cloud Authority · CI/CD Control Room
summary: A delivery pairing session predicts each automation transition before producing the first working environment and federated identity policy for deployment environments, oidc, workload identity, and cloud authority. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of environment and federated identity policy while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use named environments for approvals, branch and tag policies, scoped secrets, wait rules, URL evidence, and deployment history.
Request id-token write only in the deployment job and validate issuer, audience, subject, workflow ref, repository identity, event, and environment claims.
Exchange GitHub OIDC identity through a constrained Google Workload Identity Provider and service account without storing a key.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic workflow, policy, artifact, and release evidence review only.
# They never call GitHub APIs, start runners, execute learner commands, access Docker or registries, exchange OIDC tokens, or contact cloud services.
workspace: cicd
version-baseline:
  checkout: 7.0.0
  setup-node: 7.0.0
  upload-artifact: 7.0.1
  download-artifact: 8.0.1
  attest: 4.1.1
  node: 24-lts
  typescript-native: 7.0.2
  typescript-api-compatibility: 6.0.2
  build-push-action: 7
  google-auth: 3
  deploy-cloudrun: 3
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-delivery-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Ci Environment Protection Secret Scope
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop deployment stores a long-lived cloud key, requests broad id-token and repository permissions, and trusts ambiguous OIDC claims. Constrain environment, audience, subject, workflow, revision, service account, approval, and audit evidence. Competency probe: Use named environments for approvals, branch and tag policies, scoped secrets, wait rules, URL evidence, and deployment history.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Ci Auth Lifetime Audit Revocation
steps.instruction:
Append the CI/CD evidence item headed "  # Evidence: ci-auth-lifetime-audit-revocation-1bpc9j". Tailor its federated-deployment.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic workflow or release review, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run GitHub Actions, runner, Docker, registry, OIDC, Google Cloud, and deployment work later in authorized disposable transfer environments.

Evidence target: Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Ci Auth Lifetime Audit Revocation.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Ci Auth Lifetime Audit Revocation into observable performance evidence. For Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Ci Auth Lifetime Audit Revocation, keep Ci Auth Lifetime Audit Revocation connected to mechanically reviewable workflow, trust, artifact, deployment, and recovery evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: ci-auth-lifetime-audit-revocation-1bpc9j
  - competency: ci-auth-lifetime-audit-revocation
    artifact: federated-deployment.yaml
    decision: auth lifetime audit revocation
    environment: sandbox Google Cloud project with a private Cloud Run service
    fault-injection: cancel an older run while its external mutation is in progress
    retained-proof: lock hash cold install test report package contents and output digest
    case-key: 1bpc9j
    change: |
      environment: production
      reviewers: operations-and-security
      branch-policy: protected-main
      permissions: contents-read-id-token-write
      oidc-issuer: token.actions.githubusercontent.com
      oidc-audience: google-wif-provider
      subject: immutable-repository-workflow-environment
      google-auth-action: 3
      service-account: cloud-run-deployer
      roles: resource-scoped
      credential-lifetime: job-bounded
    verify: inspect environment history OIDC claims provider condition service-account impersonation IAM audit and credential cleanup
    failure: Reject long-lived keys, repository-name-only trust, global admin, approval-free environments, or short lifetime as sufficient control.
    repair: Constrain environment and claims, federate identity, separate service accounts, scope roles, audit exchange, expire credentials, and rehearse revocation.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.
